knowledge.html:
Nhung động từ khuyet thieu thường dùng

CAN vs BE ABLE TO

"be able to" và "can" deu diễn tả một khả năng hay sự có thể. Tuy nhien, "can" không có đang tương lai nên ta sử dụng "will be able to".

CANBE ABLE TO
Dùng để diễn tả những điều có thể làm do khả năng, năng khiếu của bạn than.
Ví dụ: I can swim. (= I have the ability to swim).
Dùng để diễn tả những điều có thể làm do có gang, xoay sở moi làm được.
Ví dụ: In spite of his broken leg, he was able to get out of the burning house.

MUST vs HAVE TO

Ca "must" và "have to" deu có nghia là "can phải/phải".

MUSTHAVE TO
Diễn tả sự cần thiết phải làm gi nhưng là do chủ quan (từ bạn than nhận thức thay).
Ví dụ: I must phone my sister. (=> I am aware that this is necessary).
Diễn tả sự cần thiết phải làm gi nhưng là do khách quan (nội quy, quy định...).
Ví dụ: Students have to go to school on time. (=> It's school's regulation).

NEED (can)

"Need" vua là động từ thường, vua là động từ khuyet thieu.

Need là động từ thườngNeed là động từ khuyet thieu
- No phải dùng tro động từ khi thành lập câu phủ định và nghi vấn.
- Động từ theo sau no phải dùng đang "to infinitive".
Ví dụ: She needs to see you.
She doesn't need to see you.
- Khi là động từ khuyet thieu NEED chỉ có hình thức hiện tại và có đây du đặc tính của một động từ khuyet thieu.
Ví dụ: Need he work so hard?
You needn't go yet, need you?

MUSTN'T vs NEEDN'T

MUSTN'TNEEDN'T
MUSTN'T (không được phep): mang y cấm đoán.
Ví dụ: You mustn't drink it. It is poisonous.
NEEDN'T (không cần thiết): mang ý nghĩa không bắt buộc.
Ví dụ: You needn't hurry. We still have a lot of time to do it.

SHOULD vs OUGHT TO

"should" và "ought to" deu có nghia là "nên" dùng để đưa ra lời khuyên, ý kiến.

SHOULDOUGHT TO
Chi sự bắt buộc hay bổn phận nhưng ở muc do nhẹ hon "Must".
Ví dụ: You should send this report by 8th September.
Chi sự bắt buộc. Manh hon "Should" nhưng chưa bang "Must".
Ví dụ: She really ought to apologize.

SHALL vs WILL

"shall" và "will" có nghia là "sẽ" dùng để đưa ra một quyết định tại thời điểm nói. "will" có thể di với tất cả các ngôi, còn "shall" chỉ được dùng với ngôi I/we.

SHALLWILL
- Dùng để xin ý kiến, đưa gợi ý.
- Người ta dùng cấu trúc "Shall I..." để đề nghị giup ai.
Ví dụ: Shall I carry the luggage for you?
- Diễn đạt, dự đoán sự việc xảy ra trong tương lai.
- Người ta dùng cấu trúc "Will you..." để đề nghị ai giup minh.
Ví dụ: Will you give me her address?

MAY vs MIGHT

"may" và "might" deu có nghia là "có le" dùng để diễn tả điều gi có thể xảy ra nhưng không chac.

MAYMIGHT
Diễn tả điều gi có thể xảy ra ở hiện tại.Diễn tả điều gi có thể xảy ra ở quá khứ. "Might" được dùng không phải là quá khứ của "may" với muc do "có thể" thấp hon "may".

CAN vs COULD

"can" và "could" được dùng trong câu hoi đề nghị, xin phep, yêu cầu.

CANCOULD
Diễn tả khả năng hiện tại hoặc tương lai mà một người có thể làm được gi, hoặc một sự việc có thể xảy ra.
Ví dụ: I can swim. / It can rain.
Diễn tả khả năng xảy ra trong quá khứ.
Ví dụ: My brother could speak English when he was five.

exercises.question: The children _______ spend too much time playing computer games.
exercises.explanation: 'Shouldn't' = should not, advising against something. 'Ought to not' is grammatically wrong (should be 'ought not to').

exercises.question: In case you're suffering from the injury, you _______ see the doctor today.
exercises.explanation: 'Had better' gives strong advice about a specific situation. It fits naturally here.

exercises.question: We _______ stop when traffic lights are red.
exercises.explanation: Traffic laws are obligations - 'must' expresses strong necessity/rules.

exercises.question: Remember to bring your raincoat. It _______ rain.
exercises.explanation: 'Might' expresses possibility - it's not certain but possible.

exercises.question: I _______ find my own way there. You _______ wait for me.
exercises.explanation: 'Can' = ability to find way; 'needn't' = it's not necessary to wait.

exercises.question: All students _______ wear uniforms at school because it is a rule.
exercises.explanation: Per Murphy Unit 31, 'have to' is used for external obligations like rules/regulations imposed by others (e.g., school rules). 'Must' is for personal feelings of necessity from the speaker. 'Because it is a rule' explicitly signals an external obligation, so 'have to' is correct.

exercises.question: You _______ finish your homework before you go to bed.
exercises.explanation: 'Should' gives advice about what is good to do - appropriate for homework advice.

exercises.question: This drink isn't beneficial for health. You _______ drink it too much.
exercises.explanation: 'Ought not to' is the correct negative form of 'ought to', giving advice against doing something.

exercises.question: This warning sign indicates that you _______ step on the grass.
exercises.explanation: A warning sign indicates prohibition - 'mustn't' = not allowed.

exercises.question: I think you _______ do exercise regularly in order to keep your body in good shape.
exercises.explanation: 'I think you should/ought to' both work for giving advice. Both B and C are correct.

exercises.question: I will lend you some money, but you _______ pay it back to me next week.
exercises.explanation: 'Must' expresses a strong personal obligation set by the speaker.

exercises.question: Hoa _______ feed the cats because her mother has done it already.
exercises.explanation: Mother already did it, số it's not necessary - 'doesn't have to'.

exercises.question: Those audiences _______ show their tickets before entering the concert hall.
exercises.explanation: Showing tickets is a requirement/regulation - 'have to' for external obligation.

exercises.question: You're having a stomachache. You had better _______ to the doctor.
exercises.explanation: 'Had better' is followed by bare infinitive (V without 'to').

exercises.question: You _______ ring the bell because I have a key.
exercises.explanation: 'Needn't' = it's not necessary, because the speaker has a key.

exercises.question: It's a hospital. You _______ smoke here.
exercises.explanation: Smoking in a hospital is prohibited - 'mustn't' = forbidden.

exercises.question: Water park is free for kids under 6 years old, so you _______ pay money for your son.
exercises.explanation: It's free, số it's not necessary to pay - 'don't have to'.

exercises.question: You look totally exhausted. You _______ take a rest instead of working overtime.
exercises.explanation: 'Should' gives advice. 'Ought' alone is incomplete (needs 'to'), 'has better' is wrong form.

exercises.question: Anyone _______ have a passport, even a visa when travelling all around the world.
exercises.explanation: Passports are a legal requirement for international travel - 'has to' (external obligation, agrees with 'anyone').

exercises.question: In the peak season, travellers _______ book their accommodation in advance.
exercises.explanation: This is advice, not an obligation - 'should' is appropriate.

exercises.wrongSentence: You needn't solve a problem until you identify it correctly.
exercises.correctSentence: You can't solve a problem until you identify it correctly.
exercises.explanation: The meaning is 'you are not able to solve' - use 'can't', not 'needn't'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Be careful with the knife! It's very sharp, you must cut your finger.
exercises.correctSentence: Be careful with the knife! It's very sharp, you might cut your finger.
exercises.explanation: 'Might' expresses possibility of an accident, not certainty.

exercises.wrongSentence: Pam can't have cooked since we ordered some pizzas.
exercises.correctSentence: Pam needn't have cooked since we ordered some pizzas.
exercises.explanation: 'Needn't have cooked' = it wasn't necessary to cook (because we ordered pizzas).

exercises.wrongSentence: They said that you shouldn't use your mobile phone at the cinema.
exercises.correctSentence: They said that you mustn't use your mobile phone at the cinema.
exercises.explanation: Using phones in cinemas is prohibited, not just advised against - 'mustn't'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Henry, will I borrow your camera tonight?
exercises.correctSentence: Henry, can I borrow your camera tonight?
exercises.explanation: Asking permission uses 'can/could/may', not 'will'.

exercises.wrongSentence: You don't must park your car here. It's a no-parking zone.
exercises.correctSentence: You mustn't park your car here. It's a no-parking zone.
exercises.explanation: 'Must' doesn't use 'do/does' for negation. The negative form is 'mustn't', not 'don't must'.

exercises.wrongSentence: She hasn't eaten anything since yesterday. She can be very hungry.
exercises.correctSentence: She hasn't eaten anything since yesterday. She must be very hungry.
exercises.explanation: 'Must' expresses logical deduction/certainty. If she hasn't eaten, she must be hungry.

exercises.wrongSentence: His teacher was very angry with him. He mustn't have said that to her.
exercises.correctSentence: His teacher was very angry with him. He shouldn't have said that to her.
exercises.explanation: 'Shouldn't have said' = it was wrong of him to say it. 'Mustn't have' is not standard.

exercises.wrongSentence: You don't have to cheat on the exam. It's against the rules.
exercises.correctSentence: You mustn't cheat on the exam. It's against the rules.
exercises.explanation: 'Don't have to' means it's not necessary. But cheating is forbidden, so use 'mustn't' (= not allowed).

exercises.wrongSentence: You might try the cheese souffle. It's really good.
exercises.correctSentence: You should try the cheese souffle. It's really good.
exercises.explanation: 'Should' is better for making a recommendation. 'Might' is too weak here.
